reviews.review_msg: Lectures are quite dry and he took out majority of the math. Wastes time in lecture answering pointless questions with little relevance to econ. Explanations were quite lacking or convoluted. Added in lectures that were not originally in the syllabus. HW marking standards were weird. Exams were ok but he could forget to mark yours. Nice guy tho.
Funny guy, engages with students. Explained concepts well, if a little bit slowly. Tests and final were fair. All around great prof.

Eliav can be unorganized sometimes, but he is very kind. TA's for this class can be unhelpful sometimes, but I noticed the course was based around his lectures so quizzes we're easy, practical and fair. Midterm and final wasn't horrible just make sure you review.

Macroeconomics was not an easy course, however, it was not difficult to achieve a high grade in this class. he gave us problem sets that are easy to get 100% on if you try, because they are not marked for correct answers but for completion. We also had quizzes that were a little bit tough but doable and there were no surprises on his exams.

Currently taking Econ 105 with him and he is a great professor. He has been really helpful and really wants his students to do well. Lectures are a bit boring, but only because most 3 hour lectures are boring in general, so you can't blame him. Glad I took his class

He is good but he is very disorganized and his exams do not cover everything from lectures so some questions are confusing. However, he gives good participation marks and show your work marks which I like and he seems nice. However, he can be intimidating to approach.

He seems to have changed the material he teaches so it has become so much easier. He has a good sense of humour and makes lectures go fast and even let people go sooner when he finishes the lecture one hour early. The course has similar things to Econ 103 so if you liked it, take this course.

Doesn't use Canvas for grades, so you're completely lost for that. Gets distracted in lectures, quizzes are okay, but exams are insane. One of his TA's is impatient and rude. His final is accumulative, so you have to remember formulas from his first lecture. Dude is completely disorganized, do not take his class.

Final exam is totally not relevant to the material. Is all about your imagination, his lecture won't help, his practice exam won't help, his assignments won't help. I you want four midterms and an F take Econ392 with Eliav Danzige. The worse professor ever.

This was one of the most disorganized classes I've ever taken. Eliav doesn't even use canvas, so I'll have no idea where I stand in this CURVED class until I get my final mark. Additionally, he doesn't even have a suggested textbook so I couldn't pre-read before class without searching through a random Macroeconomics textbook to find the topics.
